channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bureau of Corrections re-arrests Sablayan Prison escapee in Sta. Lucia
item.description: The Bureau of Corrections (BuCor) reported the re-arrest of a person deprived of liberty (PDL) who escaped from the Sablayan Prison and Penal Farm. The PDL was apprehended early Thursday morning at ar...

item.title: Tennis Star Aryna Sabalenka Gets Engaged to Oakberry CEO Georgios Frangulis
item.description: Belarusian tennis world number one Aryna Sabalenka has announced her engagement to Brazilian entrepreneur Georgios Frangulis after months of playful hints. The couple, who began dating in early 2024 a...

item.title: China Announces Lowest Economic Growth Target in Decades, Citing Domestic and Global Challenges
item.description: China has set its lowest economic growth target in decades, aiming for 4.5 to 5 per cent growth this year. Premier Li Qiang outlined the target during the opening of the National People’s Congress, ci...

item.title: ISWAP Militants Attack Borno Military Base, Army Major and Soldiers Killed
item.description: Dem wahala don burst for Borno State as ISWAP militants don attack military base for Banki junction along Bama–Gwaza Road. E happen for Sunday morning, around 4am, as the gunmen come in multiple group...

item.title: Nepal Appoints Sushila Karki as Interim PM Following Gen-Z Protests Ahead of Early Elections
item.description: In September 2025, Nepal witnessed its most violent political unrest in years after the government led by PM KP Sharma Oli banned major social media platforms including Instagram, WhatsApp, and Facebo...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV's Visit to Cameroon Sparks Political Concerns
item.description: Pope Leo XIV is set to visit Cameroon from 13 to 23 April 2026, as part of his African tour including stops in Angola and Equatorial Guinea. The visit comes shortly after Easter and ahead of legislati...

item.title: Escalation in Middle East: Israeli Strikes and Kurdish Offensive in Iran
item.description: The conflict between Israel, the United States, and Iran has intensified as Kurdish forces reportedly launched a ground offensive into western Iran, aiming to challenge the Iranian regime. Thousands o...

item.title: Apple introduces more affordable MacBook models
item.description: Apple has recently unveiled its latest line of MacBooks designed to be more budget-friendly, aiming to reach a wider range of consumers. The new models, reportedly equipped with updated hardware and p...

item.title: US Lawmakers Seek Investigation Into Risks of Side-Channel Surveillance on Modern Devices
item.description: Two US lawmakers, Senator Ron Wyden and Representative Shontel Brown, are urging a federal investigation into the risk that modern computers, smartphones, and other devices could be monitored using a ...

item.title: Iyabo Obasanjo Endorses Tinubu's Economic Policies and Declares 2027 Ogun Governorship Bid
item.description: Senator Iyabo Obasanjo, daughter of former President Olusegun Obasanjo, has publicly supported President Bola Tinubu's economic reforms, stating that they have brought positive changes in the last two...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Warns of Heavy Rainfall and Flood Risk in Nairobi Region
item.description: Nairobi and neighbouring counties are currently experiencing heavy rainfall, with the Kenya Meteorological Department issuing a warning of heightened flood risks. The most intense rains are expected b...

item.title: Kenya to Lead Expanded UN Mission in Haiti to Tackle Gang Violence
item.description: Kenya has earned rare international recognition after the UN Security Council approved the deployment of a 5,550-strong Gang Suppression Force (GSF) to stabilise Haiti. This follows the struggles of t...

item.title: How Federal and Preferential Rules Shape Police Leadership Appointments in Nigeria
item.description: Disu Rilwan Olatunji don dey make waves since him become Acting Inspector-General of Police on 25 February. Babafemi A. Badejo yan say Disu get correct academic knowledge and operational experience we...

item.title: Constitutional Court rules that antenuptial contracts signed after customary marriage are invalid, affirming in community of property regime
item.description: A recent Constitutional Court judgment in the case VVC v JRM and Others [2026] ZACC 2, handed down on 21 January 2026, has significant implications for many South African couples who entered into cust...

item.title: Conflict in the Middle East Escalates as Israel and Iran Exchange Strikes
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East continues to escalate, with Israel conducting airstrikes on Iran for the sixth consecutive day and also targeting Lebanon. Explosions were reported in Tehran as the Isr...

item.title: Labour MPs challenge proposed U.K. immigration reforms amid internal party disagreement
item.description: Over 100 Labour Party MPs have written to Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood urging a reconsideration of proposed changes to the U.K.’s immigration system. The reforms would make it harder for low-income ...

item.title: Jane Gordon Shares Personal Journey of Stroke Recovery in Her 60s
item.description: In October, Jane Gordon, a journalist and author in her late 60s, experienced an unexpected stroke, despite leading an active life and feeling mentally agile. Her day involved caring for elderly clien...

item.title: Nitish Kumar likely to shift to Rajya Sabha as BJP prepares to appoint its first Chief Minister in Bihar
item.description: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar is reportedly preparing to move to the Rajya Sabha, a development that could lead to the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) appointing its own Chief Minister in the state f...

item.title: PAA Clarifies Pakistan's Airspace Fully Open, Rejects Reports of Partial Closure Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: The Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA) has strongly denied media reports claiming partial closure of Pakistan's airspace for commercial flights due to security concerns from the US-Iran conflict. In an...

item.title: Countries boycott Milan Cortina Paralympics opening ceremony amid geopolitical tensions
item.description: The 2026 Winter Paralympics opening ceremony in Milan Cortina, scheduled for Friday, faces boycotts from several countries due to ongoing geopolitical tensions. Nations including the Czech Republic, F...
